t_ch: 上至仙舟运行航路，庙堂治策，下到行军折冲，民生问题，都由太卜司推演占算，趋吉避凶。
t_en: From navigation to governance, military issues to civil affairs, every decision on the Xianzhou must be reviewed by the Divination Commission to ensure that it can be executed without a hiccup.

t_ch: 符玄会不会出尔反尔？
t_en: Will Fu Xuan go back on her word?

t_ch: 不知道呢～那位大人行事端看占算结果，人情世故理会得少些。
t_en: Who knows? She tends to make decisions based on the results of her divinations as opposed to what others might be thinking.

t_ch: 既然她不愿示秘，只能耐心等候回音啦～
t_en: If she doesn't want to reveal the commission's secrets, then you have no choice but to wait.

t_ch: 卡芙卡就可以知道秘密了？
t_en: How come Kafka gets to know their secrets?

t_ch: 把犯人带进去就没问题吗…
t_en: But it's okay for a wanted fugitive to find out...?

t_ch: 将军之所以请太卜审问犯人，正是因为相信她能掌控局面呀。能登上「六御」之位，总有些厉害本事。
t_en: The general entrusted the master diviner with the interrogation of the fugitive because he has faith in her ability to manage the situation. As one of the Six Charioteers, she is certainly capable.

t_ch: 要不然在迴星港，那位星核猎手也不会一见她就缴枪投降啦。
t_en: Why do you think Kafka surrendered at the mere sight of her back in Stargazer Navalia?

t_ch: 停云小姐熟悉这里吗？
t_en: Miss Tingyun, are you familiar with this place?

t_ch: 怎么？恩公想要约我共游「长乐天」吗？嘻嘻……
t_en: Oh? Did you want a tour of Exalting Sanctum? Heh heh...

t_ch: 上一次驾驶星槎来此…让我想想…是约莫二十年前了。小女子终日在商路漂泊，仙舟里的景象倒有点陌生。
t_en: The last time I came here on a Starskiff was... let me think... about <unbreak>20</unbreak> years ago now. I've been on the road for so long that I've started to forget the sights and sounds of the Xianzhou...

t_ch: 只记得这儿是个闲适度日的好地方～不过，星核降临，许多洞天都变得面目全非了吧。
t_en: All I remember is that this is a good place to while away the days~ Still, many delves have changed beyond recognition since the arrival of the Stellaron.

t_ch: 没什么了…
t_en: Just taking a stroll...

t_ch: 恩公若是闲不住，可以到处逛逛，也可以这就动身和太卜司使者碰头。
t_en: If you're bored, you could go and find the messenger from the Divination Commission.

t_ch: 恩公若是闲不住，可以到处逛逛，也可以这就动身和太卜司使者碰头。
t_en: If you're bored, you could go and find the messenger from the Divination Commission.

t_ch: 「事涉秘密，无法公开」…这是不是仙舟的格言啊？从天舶司到太卜司，个个都把这话挂在嘴边，也就将军大人和蔼可亲。
t_en: "...the nature of which is confidential and cannot be disclosed..." Is that a Xianzhou motto or something? First the Sky-Faring Commission, now the Divination Commission — they're all so secretive! The general's the only friendly face here.

t_ch: 说句不想承认的话：我都有点想念桑博了…慢着！这想法是怎么冒出来的？我疯了吗？不不不，仙舟人比桑博可强太多了！
t_en: I hate to admit it, but I'm even starting to miss Sampo... Hold up! I must be losing my mind. Nope — I'll take the Xianzhou over that blue-haired bore any day...

t_ch: 唉，在贝洛伯格被追得上奔下跑，连喘口气的功夫都没有。这次到了仙舟上，时间倒是一大把，都花在等待上了。
t_en: *sigh* We've gone from being chased through every street in Belobog, to waiting on every corner in the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 追逐、逃离和等待，也许人生就是这样……
t_en: Chasing and waiting — maybe that's what life's all about...

t_ch: 哟，你会写诗啦？
t_en: Wow, when did you become a poet?

t_ch: 这有什么，美少女嘛，随便叹口气，呵出的忧伤都是诗。
t_en: Hmph, I'm not just a pretty face, y'know! I've got poetry on standby <unbreak>24</unbreak>/7.

t_ch: 仙舟的景色，你眼熟吗？
t_en: Does the Xianzhou scenery seem familiar to you?

t_ch: …你是问「仙舟有没有可能是我的故乡」？
t_en: ...Are you wondering if the Xianzhou is my homeworld?

t_ch: 没有，脑袋里没有噼啪闪过的回忆喔。拜托，你看我的头发，看我的眼睛，像仙舟人吗？你总归见过丹恒的吧。
t_en: No, I'm not getting any flashbacks. Please — do I look Xianzhouish to you? You must be thinking of Dan Heng.

t_ch: 走，逛街能治愈一切胡思乱想。
t_en: Come on! Let's go take a stroll and clear your head.

t_ch: 好耶，走起！
t_en: Good idea, let's go!

t_ch: 等等，什么叫胡思乱想啊！
t_en: Wait a minute — clear my head of what!?

t_ch: 咱们是在这儿等着，还是现在就去见太卜司的人？
t_en: Should we wait here, or go find the messenger from the Divination Commission?

t_ch: 我们和卡芙卡对峙时，她好像知道符玄会出现……
t_en: When we confronted Kafka, she seemed certain that Fu Xuan would show up...

t_ch: 她事先看过「剧本」。
t_en: She read the "script" in advance.

t_ch: 就像有人提醒了她。
t_en: It's like someone told her it would happen.

t_ch: 就像宿命般的邂逅。
t_en: Like a fated encounter.

t_ch: 很浪漫主义的比喻，不过…用在这里似乎不太恰当。
t_en: That's a romantic way of putting it, but I suspect the reality could be more sinister...

t_ch: 艾利欧——「星核猎手」的首领。没人知道他是谁，长什么样子，只知道星际和平公司称他为「命运的奴隶」。
t_en: The leader of the Stellaron Hunters is called Elio. No one knows who he is or what he looks like — only that the Interastral Peace Corporation calls him "Destiny's Slave."

t_ch: 这个人，传说他拥有能预见「可能性」的力量。黑塔说公司认为艾利欧能看见无限多的可能性产生的时间支流，这样的能力几近于预知未来。
t_en: It's said that he has the power to foresee "possibilities." According to Herta, the corporation believes Elio can see the temporal branching of infinite possibilities — which is very much like being able to predict the future...

t_ch: ——甚至有过之而无不及。如果传闻属实，那么星核猎手的每一步看似虚无的动作，实质上都是对「可能性」的选择。
t_en: ...in fact, it may even be superior. If the rumors are true, every seemingly meaningless action taken by the Stellaron Hunters may actually be a choice based on those "possibilities."

t_ch: 包括你，{NICKNAME}，他们对你做的事情也是一样的……
t_en: ...which would include what they did to you, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 我不一样，我有自由意志。
t_en: I'm different. I have free will.

t_ch: 我想这大概就是星核猎手经常在你身边出现的原因。
t_en: I think that may be why the Stellaron Hunters won't leave you alone.

t_ch: 你要小心，{NICKNAME}，猎手的每一次出现或许都是暗示，为了修正未来的方向……
t_en: Be careful, {NICKNAME}. Every time a Stellaron Hunter appears, their objective may be to steer the direction of the future.

t_ch: 我只是未来的一个变量吗？
t_en: Is that all I am — a future variable?

t_ch: 就刚刚那个话题吗？我认为没错。但，{NICKNAME}…谁又不是呢？
t_en: For the sake of this discussion? Yes, I think so. But, {NICKNAME}... who isn't?

t_ch: 我们所有人都是未来的变量，藉由选择将现实固化。未来并不是什么了不起的事情，它只是一团等待我们决定的迷雾。
t_en: All of us are future variables, our choices solidifying reality. The future is nothing incredible either — just a fog waiting for us to make a decision.

t_ch: 随便吧，我只在乎当下。
t_en: Whatever, I only care about the present.

t_ch: 那样很好，{NICKNAME}，但你要小心：猎手的每一次出现或许都是暗示，为了修正未来的方向……
t_en: That's good, {NICKNAME}, but be careful — maybe the Stellaron Hunters showing up is a sign that they're trying to change the future...

t_ch: 回到现在的话题吧，关于卡芙卡——
t_en: Back to the matter at hand — Kafka...

t_ch: 她的投降很蹊跷…
t_en: Her surrender seemed odd...

t_ch: 她居然毫不抵抗…
t_en: She didn't resist at all...

t_ch: 这都在她预料之中。
t_en: It was all within her expectations.

t_ch: 是的，一个能洞察未来的逃犯束手就擒，说明在她眼中，事情「应该如此」。
t_en: Yes. It's strange for a fugitive to give themselves up, but a fugitive who can see the future? For her, perhaps that was the way it was always going to be...

t_ch: 她真的这么放心吗？未来的可能几乎是无限的，她如此坦然，反而让我觉得：她真的有把握证明自己和这次的星核危机无关。
t_en: ...but is she really as calm as she seems? The future holds almost infinite possibilities, after all. I suspect she has proof that she has nothing to do with the Stellaron crisis.

t_ch: 又或者，太卜司的审讯将会发生什么变故，让她能够顺利脱身……
t_en: Or perhaps something is supposed to transpire during the Divination Commission's interrogation — something that would allow her a window of escape...

t_ch: 但是，星核猎手费尽心思将我们带到这里又是为了什么？这一连串行动的背后，「命运的奴隶」到底看见了怎样的未来？
t_en: *sigh* Why did the Stellaron Hunters go to such lengths to bring us here? What is the future that Destiny's Slave sees behind this course of action?

t_ch: 瓦尔特陷入了沉思……
t_en: Welt is deep in thought...

t_ch: 抱歉，我想得入神了。如果你想走，我们这就出发。
t_en: Sorry, I'm getting a little fixated. If you want to head out, we should go this way.

t_ch: 快些将那些从别处洞天撤出来的病人妥善安置起来，稳定病况。
t_en: Hurry, find beds for the patients from the other delves and stabilize them.

t_ch: 记得，过程中一定要减少接触，免得你们的人也受那不明邪祟的侵蚀——
t_en: Remember to minimize contact — we don't want your people to succumb to the same evil...

t_ch: 是是是！咱们地衡司的人手已经倾巢而出，半个都不剩啦。倒是你们丹鼎司的人呢？怎么才来这么几个？
t_en: Yeah, yeah, yeah, the Realm-Keeping Commission is out in full strength — what about the Alchemy Commission? Why are there so few of you here?

t_ch: 是是是！咱们地衡司的人手已经倾巢而出，半个都不剩啦。倒是你们丹鼎司的人呢？怎么才来这么几个？
t_en: Yeah, yeah, yeah, the Realm-Keeping Commission is out in full strength — what about the Alchemy Commission? Why are there so few of you here?

t_ch: 别乱用成语，倾巢而出那是什么好词么？
t_en: Do you mean "out in full force"?

t_ch: 哎唷，小姑奶奶，都什么时候了你还在这跟我掰扯文化，集中精力看诊吧！
t_en: Hey, missy, does this look like a classroom to you? Concentrate on treating the patients!

t_ch: 丹鼎司是指望不上了，得尽快找些能医会药的人手来……
t_en: There must be alternate medical practitioners somewhere — the Alchemy Commission can't help us right now...

t_ch: 我、我尽力啦……
t_en: That's... the best I can do...

t_ch: 呸呸，什么丧气话！这话是你一个医生随便说的吗？
t_en: Tsk, what's that supposed to mean? Doctors don't give up that easily!

t_ch: 那我能怎么办？通往丹鼎司洞天的航路也不见了。我们这些在外执勤的医师手头缺针少药，又能做些什么？
t_en: What else am I supposed to do? The route back to the Alchemy Commission has disappeared, and we're in the field without the right medicine or supplies.

t_ch: 何况这压根就不是医经上所载的疾病，这是…魔阴身发作的前兆。
t_en: Besides, we have no record of this disease at all. These symptoms are consistent with someone about to be stricken with mara.

t_ch: 病历上写着这人不过两百余岁，刚加入云骑军，哪来魔阴身发作的道理？你…你再仔细瞧瞧？
t_en: According to his medical record, this man is barely over <unbreak>200</unbreak> years old — he only just joined the Cloud Knights. How can he possibly be mara-struck? Why don't you... take another look.

t_ch: 再这样下去，十王司的判官们该要出来收拾残局了。
t_en: If it goes on like this, the judges of the Ten-Lords Commission will have to come out and clean up this mess.

t_ch: 我长这么大还没见识过「魔阴身」的症状…没想到竟是这般可怕。
t_en: My first time seeing these symptoms up close... Being stricken with mara is more terrible than I imagined.

t_ch: 听说持明族的龙女也来了，不知道她的医术能不能镇住场子……
t_en: I heard the Vidyadhara dragon lady is here. Maybe she can help stem the tide...

t_ch: 都往后退！
t_en: Everybody, stay back!

t_ch: 快喂他喝下这药，让他乖乖躺好。
t_en: Quick! Get him to drink the cure and lay him down!

t_ch: 我是说…让他们…乖乖躺好！
t_en: I mean... Lay... all of them down!

t_ch: 那个云骑军到底怎么了？
t_en: What happened to that Cloud Knight?

t_ch: 散了散了，不要围观。
t_en: Nothing to see here folks — go home.

t_ch: 啧，真是屋漏偏逢连夜雨。工造司那厢也出了问题，还是个大麻烦！你去通知云骑军，看看能不能……
t_en: Tsk, when it rains, it pours. The Artisanship Commission has its own issues — sounds serious too! Go and inform the Cloud Knights — see if you can...

t_ch: 失礼了，在下是地衡司勤务。看您的打扮，是造访「罗浮」的外客吗？
t_en: Oh, my apologies. I'm an orderly from the Realm-Keeping Commission. Judging by your dress, you must be visitors to the Luofu?

t_ch: 是的。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 很抱歉，仙舟上目前出了些…特殊状况。但云骑军一定会尽速妥善解决的。
t_en: Sorry, the Xianzhou is currently caught in... unusual circumstances. But the Cloud Knights will resolve the issues as soon as possible.

t_ch: 出于安全考虑，请您呆在旅馆里，暂时不要外出行动。
t_en: For safety reasons, please stay in your residence and refrain from going out for the time being.

t_ch: 我是你们将军请来的客人。
t_en: I'm a guest — the general invited me.

t_ch: 我是将军请来的公务员。
t_en: And I'm a civil servant — the general hired me.

t_ch: 话也没错，严格来说，每一位抵达仙舟「罗浮」的旅客，都是受将军之邀而来的贵客。
t_en: Technically speaking, every traveler aboard the Luofu is a guest of the general.

t_ch: 如果埠口还在通航，我会建议阁下立刻坐船前往「星槎海」，不过，据说离开这儿的航路暂时停运了。
t_en: If the port is still open, I would suggest you take a boat to Starskiff Haven right away, though I heard the route may have been temporarily suspended...

t_ch: 对了，我认识个朋友，他的星槎有通行许可证，能帮您和您的朋友速速离开，价格公道好商量，您意下如何？
t_en: Oh, that reminds me... I have a friend with a starskiff and a travel permit — he could help you and your friends get over quickly. The price is fair and negotiable. What do you think?

t_ch: 不必，我有专船接送。
t_en: No need, I have a vessel picking me up.

t_ch: 唷，还真是贵客！私底下给您透个信，这次可不是小打小闹的安全维护。将军派了云骑军清查隐患。
t_en: Well now, you must be quite special! Don't spread this around, but this isn't some small-scale maintenance drill. The general has sent the Cloud Knights to check for hidden dangers.

t_ch: 虽然您不一定需要，但要是有什么急用得上我的地方，欢迎随时来找我。
t_en: You may not need it right now, but if there's any help I can give you, come to me any time.

t_ch: 不了吧。
t_en: No, thanks.

t_ch: 那行，在下还有公务要忙，您要是改了主意，欢迎随时来找我。
t_en: Hmph, very well. I still have business to attend to. If you change your mind, come to me any time.

t_ch: 唔，每个月就领这么几个钱，出了事儿却得顶上前线……谁干谁傻啊。
t_en: Ugh, they pay us next to nothing then order us straight to the front line when things go south... I must be crazy.

t_ch: 稍安勿躁，各位。遵地衡司总务府的公文，「长乐天」目前会暂时封闭关口航路。具体消息很快会发送在邸报和各位的玉兆上。
t_en: Everyone, please remain calm. This is an announcement from the Realm-Keeping Commission General Bureau. Exalting Sanctum is temporarily closing its borders and navigation routes. The details will be outlined in the official bulletin sent to your jade abacus soon.

t_ch: 若有身体不适的朋友，请速速前往地衡司公廨登记。我们请来丹鼎司的医师会在此逗留，为各位免费看诊。
t_en: If you feel unwell, please report to the Realm-Keeping Commission as soon as possible. We have doctors from the Alchemy Commission here who will see you free of charge.

t_ch: 对了，我们还会向各位发放蛋白米一袋，应急药物若干。这些物资找我当面申领，或是晚些由地衡司勤务送上门都行。
t_en: Oh, we will also be distributing a bag of protein rice and emergency medicine to each of you. You can collect the supplies from me in person, or the commission can deliver them to your door.

t_ch: 你好，我来申领物资。
t_en: Hello, I'm here to collect supplies.

t_ch: 请用您的玉兆手镯登记…哎，您是外来的旅客啊。
t_en: Please register with your jade abacus bracelet... Oh, you must be an outsider.

t_ch: 我明白了，来，您在这儿签个字。
t_en: I see. Please, sign here.

t_ch: 你好，太卜司那边情况如何？
t_en: Hello, how's the Divination Commission faring?

t_ch: 听说太卜司的穹仪出了问题。
t_en: I heard there's a problem with the meridian pin.

t_ch: 有些个卜者和观星士逃出了洞天。其中一些人害怕地嚷嚷什么「植物」、「根须」之类的话…也不知道其中究竟发生了什么可怕的事情。
t_en: Some diviners and stargazers managed to escape the delve. Many of them were terrified — they were muttering about "plants" and "roots..." Something terrible must have happened there.

t_ch: 莫非您有朋友留在那儿，还没能出来？您要是实在担心，可以去找个逃出来的卜者来问问。
t_en: Do you have friends trapped in the delve? If you're worried about them, find a diviner and see if they can tell you what happened.

t_ch: 您辛苦了。
t_en: Thanks for everything you're doing.

t_ch: 虽然我想说不辛苦…但，我已经连续在这儿站了六个时辰了。这场灾难，什么时候才能结束呢？
t_en: I want to say it's all in a day's work, but I've been here for twelve hours already... When will this end?

t_ch: 遵地衡司总务府的公文，「长乐天」目前会暂时封闭……
t_en: This is an announcement from the Realm-Keeping Commission General Bureau. Exalting Sanctum is temporarily closing its borders...

t_ch: 这位{F#小姐}{M#小哥}，请问有什么能帮你的吗？
t_en: Can I help you with anything?

t_ch: 你好，我来申领物资。
t_en: Hello, I'm here to collect supplies.

t_ch: 请用您的玉兆手镯登记……
t_en: Please register with your...

t_ch: 等等，我是不是才刚发给过你物资来着…这位客人，请不要戏弄我们地衡司的工作人员。
t_en: Wait a minute, I already issued you with supplies... You won't fool the Realm-Keeping Commission that easily.

t_ch: 您辛苦了。
t_en: Thanks for everything you're doing.

t_ch: 虽然我想说不辛苦…但，我已经连续在这儿站了六个时辰了。这场灾难，什么时候才能结束呢？
t_en: All in a day's work, although I've been standing here for twelve hours now... I just want this to be over.

t_ch: 我是不是刚才也跟谁这么抱怨过来着…？
t_en: Huh, now I'm getting déjà vu...

t_ch: …一定是累糊涂了。
t_en: ...I think I need to take a break.

t_ch: 那儿妥妥就是广场了。
t_en: That's the plaza...

t_ch: 可是，也没瞧见谁在等人呀？咱们四处找找吧？
t_en: ...but I can't see anyone waiting for us. Should we look around?

t_ch: 这是什么意思？就一张图片，是让我们去这个地方碰头吗？
t_en: What does that mean? It's just a picture. Are we supposed to meet someone there?

t_ch: 也许是遇上了什么麻烦？
t_en: Maybe there's been a problem?

t_ch: 好像电影里的绑匪接头喔……
t_en: It's like one of those messages a kidnapper sends in a horror movie...

t_ch: 别开玩笑啦，我们走吧。
t_en: That's enough of that. Come on, let's go.

t_ch: 哼哼……
t_en: Hmmmm...

t_ch: 动作快点啊，青雀。等你过这一手，咱们哥几个都快坐化了。
t_en: Hurry up, Qingque! We'll be fossils by the time you're finished.

t_ch: 今天地衡司发了公告，「星槎海」的港口也关闭了。云骑军四处出动，封锁设施。朋友们，珍惜还能打牌的好日子吧。
t_en: The Realm-Keeping Commission made an announcement today — Starskiff Haven has been closed off and the Cloud Knights are shutting everything down. We need to enjoy our celestial jade-playing days while they last!

t_ch: 听说太卜司的洞天也遭了灾？青雀，你怎么还有心思玩牌戏啊？
t_en: I heard there was a disaster in the Divination Commission's delve? I'm surprised you're in the mood to play right now, Qingque.

t_ch: 打牌也是一种定力修炼嘛，卜者可是很需要定力的。
t_en: *yawn* Celestial jade helps train my concentration. Diviners need to be able to focus.

t_ch: 太卜司的天就是塌下来，还有太卜大人顶着。虽然她老人家身高不济，能耐却是顶天的。
t_en: Even if the sky was gonna collapse on the Divination Commission, the master diviner would be there to hold it up. What she lacks in height, she more than makes up for in stamina.

t_ch: 我来这儿也不是瞎玩呀，是奉了她的命令，在此等候要来的贵客。时间多宝贵呀，这叫「摸鱼工作两不误」。
t_en: Anyway, I didn't come here just to play — she ordered me to wait here for our guests. Time is precious! I'm simply multitasking.

t_ch: 看照片应该就是这里了。这…是个牌馆？！在这儿能有什么麻烦？
t_en: It's the place in the photo alright. Is this... a games parlor!? What kind of problem can they possibly be facing?

t_ch: 哈！这牌还不麻烦吗？哎呀，这是摸了个什么鬼……
t_en: Ha! Isn't this hand a problem? I have the worst luck...

t_ch: 三位好啊！一看三位面带贵气，就知道你们准是太卜司的贵客！
t_en: Hi there! I can tell just by looking at you that you're the Divination Commission's guests.

t_ch: 把大家叫到这里，就为这事啊？
t_en: Did you bring us here just for this?

t_ch: 你知道我们等了多久吗！
t_en: Do you even know how long we've been waiting!?

t_ch: 你也不想让太卜知道你去玩牌吧？
t_en: You don't want the master diviner to know you've been slacking off, do you?

t_ch: 对不住、对不住嘛。我原本也想等你们来着…哎，那个，碰！！
t_en: I know, I know, I'm sorry — I was going to wait for you by the statue, but— Ah— Triplet!

t_ch: 只是那附近被地衡司的人占去了，实在嘈杂…吃！
t_en: ...but folks from the Realm-Keeping Commission descended on the area. It's too loud over there now... Pass! Pass!

t_ch: 我心说，要是在那样喧嚣的地方和诸位碰头，岂不是…到我了？杠！！
t_en: ...So I thought to myself, wouldn't it be better to meet somewhere quieter? FOUR OF A KIND!

t_ch: …岂不是煞风景，不如就趁着闲暇时光带各位「长乐天」一游，顺便体验一下仙舟民粹——帝垣琼玉牌。等我…这一把……
t_en: ...And wouldn't that be more fun, too? Better to show you the real Exalting Sanctum and introduce you to a true Xianzhou pastime: Celestial jade. Aha...

t_ch: 和啦！
t_en: Victory!

t_ch: 此间心愿已了，再无牵挂。客人，请，咱们出发吧。
t_en: *sigh* Now that my wish has been fulfilled, there's nothing holding us back. Follow me, honored guests.

t_ch: 让诸位贵客等我许久，青雀实在过意不去。
t_en: I must apologize for your long wait, honored guests.

t_ch: 也不算太久，在一旁看姑娘玩得热火朝天，也有些好奇这个帝垣琼玉牌。
t_en: It wasn't too long. Seeing your enthusiasm for this "celestial jade" game has stoked my curiosity.

t_ch: 嗨呀，先生说话真是耐心又体贴，还很有眼光呢！有兴趣的话，我来教教你这帝垣琼玉牌？很好玩的。
t_en: Haha, I'm glad to hear it — you clearly have excellent taste! If you're interested I can show you how to play — it's great fun.

t_ch: 好啊。
t_en: Sure!

t_ch: 杨叔，现在是办正事的时候吧！
t_en: Mr. Yang! Is this really the best time?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你们看，那是…？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, look! Is that a...?

t_ch: 那是名唤「建木」的古树，「罗浮」仙舟曾引以为傲的宝物。
t_en: It's an ancient tree — we call it the Ambrosial Arbor. It was once the Xianzhou Luofu's prized treasure.

t_ch: 天舶司的人也对历史这么有研究吗？厉害啊，新生代几乎都说不出它的来历了。据说这建木是上古仙舟遨游天外时所遗留的残迹。
t_en: I didn't know the Sky-Faring Commission were history buffs! Not bad, younger generations usually don't know much about it. It's said that the Ambrosial Arbor is an ancient remnant from when the Xianzhou roamed the ether.

t_ch: 别看远望不过是半截枯木，按《上国梦华录》里的记载，它全盛时的体积「攀揽穹窿，垂挂辰宿」！
t_en: From a distance it looks like half a tree stump, but according to Records of the Early Nation, in its prime, "it wound into the heavens, and from it celestial bodies hung!"

t_ch: 什么意思…？
t_en: Meaning...?

t_ch: 是说这棵树的高度能攀上天空，枝条上能垂下星星。
t_en: It means the tree was as tall as the sky and stars hung from its branches.

t_ch: 那得有多大？列车这么大？不对，黑塔空间站这么大！也不对。
t_en: So how big was it? Bigger than the Express? Bigger than Herta space station? No, that can't be right.

t_ch: 垂下星星…这怕是整座仙舟都装不下吧！
t_en: "Stars hung from its branches..." Then how did it fit in the Xianzhou!?

t_ch: 太空船里哪棵树都符合描述吧？
t_en: Technically, any tree in the spaceship fits this description.

t_ch: 这是修辞！修辞！不要在故事里死抠现实的字眼啊！
t_en: Those are metaphors! You shouldn't take poetry so literally.

t_ch: 真是难以想象的雄伟巨树。
t_en: It sounds like an unimaginably magnificent tree.

t_ch: 正是，总之想象有多大，它就有多大。
t_en: Exactly, let's just say it was bigger than your imagination.

t_ch: 反正都是传说罢了。我上下班路上，天天都能瞧见这般景色，看也看腻味了。
t_en: Anyway, it's just a legend. I see this view every day on my way to work — I'm kinda bored of it.

t_ch: 咱们走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 天东有若木，下置衔烛龙…有生之年，我一定能看到这番景象。
t_en: On the eastern horizon, the arbor stands tall, the candle dragon down below... One day, I'll see that view for myself.

t_ch: 这位小友，你也是来此处游赏「长乐天」胜景的吗？
t_en: Hello, are you here for a bit of Exalting Sanctum scenery too?

t_ch: 路过。
t_en: Just passing by.

t_ch: 叨扰了，请随意。
t_en: Sorry to bother you.

t_ch: 我正要赶路去太卜司。
t_en: I'm on my way to the Divination Commission.

t_ch: 在下虽然目不能视，但也听说「罗浮」上目前出了些变故。太卜司所在的洞天断绝了音信。安全起见，还是留在此处为好。
t_en: I've heard there are serious incidents unfolding on the Luofu. Communication with the Divination Commission's delve has been severed. For your own safety, I'd suggest you remain here.

t_ch: 要是有非去不可的理由，也请务必小心。
t_en: If you do have an important reason to go, then please be extra careful.

t_ch: 我不过是一介医师，帮不上您什么忙。这些是丹鼎司配发给外勤医师的丹药，也许能应急，拿上吧。
t_en: I'm just a doctor — there's not much I can do to assist you. These are medicinal pellets that the Alchemy Commission hands out to field doctors — you might find them useful in an emergency. Take them.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: That's right.

t_ch: 那么，有劳您，能否为我描述一下看到的景象？
t_en: In that case, would you mind describing it to me?

t_ch: 阁下看不见眼前的景色吗？
t_en: ...You mean you can't see it yourself?

t_ch: 抱歉。在下自出生起就目不能视，是个「天缺者」。
t_en: Precisely. I've been without sight since the day I was born.

t_ch: 我其余的感官倒也不弱于常人，能稍稍弥补所需。
t_en: My other senses make up for that fact to some extent — they're as strong as anyone else's.

t_ch: 我能听到风里飘来的人声喧闹，嗅到工造司雨云降下的湿气，触摸到玉勒花瓣细腻的质感。但独独缺一双眼睛……
t_en: I can hear the sounds of people on the wind, smell the moisture from the rain clouds of the Artisanship Commission, feel the delicate texture of the yule flower's petals. All I lack is a window on the world...

t_ch: 我这辈子来过这里许多次了，只能白白面对着那边的景色兴叹。
t_en: I've been here many times in my life, but the scenery can never fully reveal itself to me.

t_ch: 能否劳烦您，为我用语言描述一下那番景象？
t_en: That's why I'd love you to describe it to me.

t_ch: 我明白了。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 抱歉，我还有要事在身。
t_en: I'm so sorry, but I have something important to attend to.

t_ch: 叨扰了，请随意。
t_en: Sorry to bother you.

t_ch: 就快到啦，各位跟紧。
t_en: We're almost there everyone, stay close.

t_ch: 刚才吓死我了，多亏有人救了那位持明族的龙女大人……
t_en: That was terrifying, thank goodness the Vidyadhara dragon lady was saved...

t_ch: 是啊，那孩子平日里都深居简出，怎么会跑到咱们这儿来。
t_en: Yes, that child's usually hidden away. What's she doing out here?

t_ch: 听说沾染了邪祟的云骑军都发起狂了！
t_en: I heard the Knights affected by the evil are going berserk!

t_ch: 别吓唬自己，有丹鼎司的龙女在，什么疑难杂症都能治好。
t_en: Don't scare yourself — the Alchemy Commission's dragon lady can cure anything.

t_ch: 这位{M#小兄弟}{F#小妹妹}打搅了，请留步。
t_en: Excuse me, {M#fella}{F#miss} — hold on a moment.

t_ch: 之前幸好有您出手，替「长乐天」的百姓和那位龙女大人解了围。
t_en: It was only thanks to your help earlier that the dragon lady and those Exalting Sanctum residents got away unscathed.

t_ch: 龙女大人？
t_en: Dragon lady?

t_ch: 就是那位丹鼎司的龙女，白露大人。
t_en: The dragon lady of the Alchemy Commission — Lady Bailu.

t_ch: 那个孩子啊，听称呼来头还不小。
t_en: You mean the child? She's starting to sound like a big shot.

t_ch: 解了围？
t_en: Our help?

t_ch: 真是贵人忘事。您在危急关头出手摆平了魔阴身，在下可都看在眼里。
t_en: You don't remember? You dealt with those mara-struck knights in the nick of time — I saw it all.

t_ch: 我从天舶司的朋友那儿听说将军请来了几位客人，似乎专为解决罗浮之上的麻烦而来。看来就是你们几位了。
t_en: A friend in the Sky-Faring Commission told me the general commissioned guests to deal with the troubles on the Luofu. You must be the guests.

t_ch: 如果您不介意的话，希望能拨冗来地衡司公廨一叙。我想和您聊聊「长乐天」眼下面临的麻烦……
t_en: If you don't mind, I hope you can take the time to come to the Realm-Keeping Commission. I'd like to talk to you about the issues Exalting Sanctum is currently facing.

t_ch: 眼下四周嘈杂，不便细说。如果您不介意的话，希望能拨冗来地衡司公廨一叙。
t_en: This isn't a good place to talk — too much activity. If you don't mind, I hope you can come to the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 被人教训了一顿，现在能乖乖躺好了吧？
t_en: ...That'll teach you a lesson. Ready to lie down now?

t_ch: 多谢{F#叔叔姐姐}{M#哥哥姐姐}帮忙稳定病患的情绪啦，这一手「重击麻醉」技术相当了得啊。
t_en: Thanks for helping to stabilize the patients — your "assertive sedation" techniques are quite effective.

t_ch: 「重击麻醉」…我们有吗？
t_en: "Assertive sedation techniques..." Does she mean beating people up?

t_ch: 不过你们这一出手，这几个云骑病上加伤，我得加把劲给他们正骨包扎一下。可恶，这下完全忙不过来了啊！
t_en: However, these Cloud Knights were already sick, and now they're injured too. I've gotta bandage up their wounds, realign their bones... Ugh, as if I didn't have enough on my plate!

t_ch: 小朋友，你在给他们看病？
t_en: Aren't you a little young to be realigning bones?

t_ch: 看病可不是小孩子过家家…
t_en: I'm gonna need to see some qualifications...

t_ch: 我明白，你们瞧我身材小小，就觉得我一定是背着父母偷偷跑出来的「小朋友」。
t_en: I get it — you think because I'm small I must be a runaway child.

t_ch: 哼，这里可是仙舟！外头来的短生种{F#小妹妹}{M#小老弟}，可别以貌取人啊！咱们持明族轮回自足，不需要什么爸爸妈妈！
t_en: Hmph, welcome to the Xianzhou my short-lived outsider friends — appearances can be deceiving here! The Vidyadhara race is self-reincarnating — no mum or dad required!

t_ch: 本小姐打从蜕生出水就开始研习医道了，在丹鼎司里也是正儿八经的挂牌执业的医士！
t_en: I've been studying the art of healing ever since I cast off my old shell! You're looking at a recognized, practicing, dedicated doctor.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格的孩子还在地底下玩泥巴，这里的孩子就已经能熟练地给人看病了。
t_en: Belobog kids are making snowmen while children here are writing prescriptions...

t_ch: 仙舟联盟除去人类与狐人定居之外，还有被称为「龙裔」的持明一族。这孩子应该就是其中的一员吧。
t_en: In addition to humans and foxians, the Xianzhou Alliance is also home to the Vidyadhara people — also known as "Long's Scions." This child must be one of them.

t_ch: 最近罗浮上很不太平呀，你们如果……
t_en: Things haven't been very peaceful on the Luofu recently. Make sure you don't—

t_ch: 如果没事就别到处乱跑是吧？真遗憾，罗浮的将军给了咱们一桩差事，少不得要跑东跑西。
t_en: Go running around, right? Well, your general gave us an errand, so I'm afraid we have to.

t_ch: 那，{F#叔叔姐姐们}{M#哥哥姐姐}要是有什么三长两短、跌打损伤的…我可以为你们免费看诊！看在你们救了我的份上，药金就打个八折吧。
t_en: Hm, well, seeing as you saved me just now, if you're unlucky enough to acquire any breaks or sprains... I'll treat you for free! As for medicine, I'll give you a twenty percent discount.

t_ch: 不是说免费看诊吗？
t_en: That doesn't sound very "free."

t_ch: 结果还要收钱啊！
t_en: So you're still charging us!

t_ch: 哈，你知道罗浮仙舟上有多少人想挂个号看本小姐的专家门诊，却求之不得嘛！
t_en: Ha, do you have any idea how many people on the Luofu are waiting for an appointment with me? You should count this as a blessing!

t_ch: 要不是溜出来的时候身上没带够钱，本小姐也不至于……
t_en: If I hadn't left my purse at home, I wouldn't have to...

t_ch: 咳咳，闲话打住，误了病人的诊断可就不妙啦。
t_en: Ahem, enough of that. I have patients to see.

t_ch: 这孩子哪来的？你爸爸呢？
t_en: Where did you come from little one? Is your Dad around?

t_ch: 我没爸爸。
t_en: I don't have a dad.

t_ch: 那你妈妈呢？
t_en: Uh — what about your mom?

t_ch: 我也没妈妈。
t_en: I don't have a mom either.

t_ch: 杨叔，这位小神医看起来和周遭的人似乎完全不是一个画风啊。
t_en: Our little miracle worker here seems very different from everyone else around — don't you think Mr. Yang?

t_ch: 原来如此，这便是「持明族」吗？我也只在文献里看过记录。听说他们是龙脉族裔，如此一看确实很贴切。
t_en: So this is the Vidyadhara, I've only ever read about them. They're known as the dragon race... not hard to see why.

t_ch: 本小姐要专心救人
t_en: I need to focus on healing

t_ch: 不能分心
t_en: I can't be distracted

t_ch: 你们丹鼎司的人呢？
t_en: Where are the people from the Alchemy Commission?

t_ch: 洞天被关闭了
t_en: The delve is closed off

t_ch: 人手都在这儿了
t_en: Everyone we can mobilize is here

t_ch: 我、我尽力啦
t_en: I... I tried my best

t_ch: 别胡说！
t_en: Don't be ridiculous!

t_ch: 航路暂时封闭
t_en: The route is temporarily closed

t_ch: 我们会向各位发放物资
t_en: We will be distributing supplies to everyone

t_ch: 建木…建木重生了！
t_en: Ambro... The Ambrosial Arbor is reborn!

t_ch: 这怎么可能？
t_en: How could this happen?

t_ch: 我刚听到的响动是…
t_en: What I heard just now...

t_ch: 你看见了吗…那棵树…
t_en: Did you see that... that tree?

t_ch: 何其壮观的神迹…
t_en: What a spectacular view...

t_ch: 便是长生种也难得一见
t_en: That is true even for long-life species

t_ch: 卜卦的结果是凶
t_en: The result of the augury is woe

t_ch: 还好我支起了换境画屏
t_en: Thank fortunes I put up the shifting screen

t_ch: 惨了，师傅他…
t_en: This is bad, master, he...

t_ch: 这下毕不了业了…
t_en: I won't be able to graduate at this rate...

t_ch: 师姐，这材质很奇特啊
t_en: Yunche, this material is so unique

t_ch: 唉，傻师弟，别去送死
t_en: Silly Ziming, don't go seeking your own demise

t_ch: 刚刚那四个人什么来头
t_en: Who are those four people

t_ch: 谁知道呢？
t_en: Who knows?

t_ch: 也许是逃难的游客吧？
t_en: Maybe they are visitors who are fleeing?

t_ch: 与此同时，在罗浮的另一边…
t_en: Concurrently, at the other end of the Luofu...

t_ch: 出示玉符
t_en: Show the jade seal

t_ch: 这「流云渡」的运货通道若是稍有变化，整个路线就截然不同了。
t_en: If the shipping passages in Cloudford changed even a little, the entire route would be drastically altered.

t_ch: 两位认得路吗？
t_en: Do you two know the way?

t_ch: 我不认得。
t_en: I do not.

t_ch: 哈哈，本姑娘就是从这儿来的，熟得很，跟我走就没错啦。
t_en: Haha, I came this way, I know it like the back of my hand. Just follow me.

t_ch: 那个…闷葫芦，你准备好了就跟我说一声，咱们好出发。
t_en: Um... So, silent but deadly, let me know when you're ready and we can set off.

t_ch: …还有这种事？我也刚来「罗浮」……
t_en: ...Seriously? I just got to the Luofu myself...

t_ch: 我懂我懂，这种感觉……
t_en: I get it. This feeling...

t_ch: 丹恒兄弟是罗浮人么？
t_en: Do you hail from the Luofu, young master Dan Heng?

t_ch: 是。
t_en: I do.

t_ch: 这样啊…你不愿多说，也没关系。
t_en: I see... It's fine if you'd prefer not to discuss further.

t_ch: 不是。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 哦，不知怎的，我看你与罗浮有种极为契合的感觉…大概是我看错了吧。
t_en: Ah. For some reason, you seem to fit right in here... Perhaps I read it wrong.

t_ch: （沉默）
t_en: (Remain Silent)

t_ch: …抱歉，多此一问。
t_en: ...Apologies, forgive my curiosity.

t_ch: 我有时掌握不好与人交流的分寸，希望没有冒犯到你。
t_en: I do often struggle to judge the boundaries of small talk — I hope I haven't offended you.

t_ch: 有事请教…
t_en: I'd like to ask something...

t_ch: 请说。
t_en: Please do...

t_ch: 罗浮封锁之事，你知道多少？
t_en: How much do you know about the Luofu being sealed off?

t_ch: 几乎一无所知。我本要依照行程登船离开罗浮，突然听说爆发了骚乱。
t_en: Almost nothing. I was scheduled to leave the Luofu when I heard there was a disturbance.

t_ch: 隔壁这位素裳姑娘是云骑军人，去问她吧。
t_en: Miss Sushang here is a member of the Cloud Knights. You could try asking her.

t_ch: 这位云骑姑娘是什么人？
t_en: Who is this Cloud Knight girl?

t_ch: 她叫素裳，好像刚从仙舟「曜青」调动过来。
t_en: Her name is Sushang. She apparently just transferred here from the Yaoqing.

t_ch: 据我所知，每艘仙舟虽然独立行事，但云骑军却是由元帅统一指挥的，故而各支云骑之间常有兵将调遣，也不奇怪。
t_en: Every Xianzhou ship operates individually, but the Cloud Knights are all commanded by the Marshal. Therefore, it's normal for Knights to transfer between ships.

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 谈谈你吧。
t_en: Let's talk about you.

t_ch: 我？
t_en: Me?

t_ch: 你不像仙舟人，从哪儿来的？
t_en: You don't seem like you're from the Xianzhou. Where have you come from?

t_ch: 行商没有所属，四处漂泊，星海为家。但如果你好奇…我在联盟的注册地是仙舟「玉阙」；在公司的登记号隶属于北谷星：星和商城分部。
t_en: Merchants don't belong anywhere — we wander and make the stars our home. But if you're curious... Within the Alliance I'm registered at the Xianzhou Yuque. Within the IPC, I'm registered at the Star Unity Mall branch at the North Valley Star.

t_ch: …所以罗刹只是我在仙舟的常用名。仙舟以外的地方，我有个更拗口的名字。我猜你也想问这个吧？
t_en: ...Therefore, Luocha is just the name I use within the Xianzhou. Outside of the Xianzhou, I use something more difficult to pronounce. I'm guessing you want to know that name, too?

t_ch: 作为商人，你的武艺不错。
t_en: You have pretty good fighting skills for a merchant.

t_ch: 谁叫宇宙这么危险呢。作为行商，公司和联盟只能庇佑我们进行星际穿越而已，安保这件事可全得看自己的手段。
t_en: The universe is a dangerous place. The IPC and the Alliance only provide for our safety during interstellar travel — on-site security is dependent on our own capacities.

t_ch: 剑术和医术，都是这么练出来的。丹恒兄弟也是漂泊的人，一定能理解吧。
t_en: As such, I had to improve my swordplay and healing skills. You are also a wanderer, young master Dan Heng — surely you understand?

t_ch: 那口棺材里是什么？
t_en: What's in that coffin?

t_ch: 啊，那是一段孽缘。我不小心卷入一场争斗，平白无故担了人情，只好帮忙把一具遗体送还仙舟。
t_en: Ah, a story of ill luck. I found myself embroiled in a conflict and ended up owing a favor... Now I must assist in delivering these remains back to the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 我这人或许有些惹祸上身的本事吧，本来寻思到了罗浮总该安全了，可又出了这样的事情……
t_en: It would seem I am a magnet for such misfortune... I thought I would be safe on the Luofu, but now...

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 没事，叨扰了。
t_en: Never mind. Excuse me.

t_ch: 不会。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 不动身吗？在这里耽搁太久也不安全吧。
t_en: Are we heading off? It's not safe to linger here.

t_ch: 有事请教…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 请说。
t_en: Please. Speak your mind.

t_ch: 罗浮封锁之事，你知道多少？
t_en: How much do you know about the Luofu being sealed off?

t_ch: 几乎一无所知。我本要依照行程登船离开罗浮，突然听说爆发了骚乱。
t_en: Almost nothing. I was scheduled to leave the Luofu when I heard there was a disturbance.

t_ch: 隔壁这位素裳姑娘是云骑军人，去问她吧。
t_en: Miss Sushang here is a member of the Cloud Knights. You can try asking her.

t_ch: 这位云骑姑娘是什么人？
t_en: Who is this Cloud Knight girl?

t_ch: 她叫素裳，好像刚从仙舟「曜青」调动过来。
t_en: Her name is Sushang. She apparently just transferred here from the Yaoqing.

t_ch: 据我所知，每艘仙舟虽然独立行事，但云骑军却是由元帅统一指挥的，故而各支云骑之间常有兵将调遣，也不奇怪。
t_en: Every Xianzhou ship operates individually, but the Cloud Knights are all commanded by the Marshal. Therefore, it's normal for Knights to transfer between ships.

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 谈谈你吧。
t_en: Let's talk about you.

t_ch: 我？
t_en: Me?

t_ch: 你不像仙舟人，从哪儿来的？
t_en: You don't seem like you're from the Xianzhou. Where have you come from?

t_ch: 行商没有所属，四处漂泊，星海为家。但如果你好奇…我在联盟的注册地是仙舟「玉阙」；在公司的登记号隶属于北谷星：星和商城分部。
t_en: Merchants do not belong anywhere. We wander and make the stars our home. But if you're curious... I am registered at the Xianzhou Yuque within the Alliance. With the IPC, I'm registered at the Star Unity Mall branch at the North Valley Star.

t_ch: …所以罗刹只是我在仙舟的常用名。仙舟以外的地方，我有个更拗口的名字。我猜你也想问这个吧？
t_en: ...Therefore, Luocha is just the name I use at Xianzhou. Outside of Xianzhou, I use something more difficult to pronounce. I'm guessing you want to know that name, too?

t_ch: 作为商人，你的武艺不错。
t_en: You have great martial skills for a merchant.

t_ch: 谁叫宇宙这么危险呢。作为行商，公司和联盟只能庇佑我们进行星际穿越而已，安保这件事可全得看自己的手段。
t_en: The universe is a dangerous place. The IPC and the Alliance can only ensure that we travel in safety. Our personal security is dependent on our own capacities.

t_ch: 剑术和医术，都是这么练出来的。丹恒兄弟也是漂泊的人，一定能理解吧。
t_en: That's how I trained my swordplay and healing skills. You are also a wanderer. Surely you understand.

t_ch: 那口棺材里是什么？
t_en: What's in that coffin?

t_ch: 啊，那是一段孽缘。我不小心卷入一场争斗，平白无故担了人情，只好帮忙把一具遗体送还仙舟。
t_en: Ahh, that was some ill luck. I got involved in a conflict by accident and ended up owing a favor, so now I have to help send a deceased body back to Xianzhou.

t_ch: 我这人或许有些惹祸上身的本事吧，本来寻思到了罗浮总该安全了，可又出了这样的事情……
t_en: Perhaps I simply attract problems... I thought I would be safe at the Luofu, but now...

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 没事，叨扰了。
t_en: Nothing. Please excuse me.

t_ch: 不会。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 闷葫芦，我这么叫你，你不会生气吧？
t_en: Silent but deadly... are you sure that nickname isn't annoying you?

t_ch: 不生气。
t_en: Not at all.

t_ch: 你性格还真好……
t_en: Huh, I should learn to be this easygoing...

t_ch: 无所谓。
t_en: I don't mind.

t_ch: 你还真豁达哎。
t_en: It's good to have a sense of humor!

t_ch: （沉默）
t_en: (Remain Silent)

t_ch: …唉，真是一点都没叫错。
t_en: *sigh* ...I mean, you're kinda living up to the first bit right now.

t_ch: 我们随时都能动身，你好了就跟我说一声。
t_en: We can move out any time. Let me know when you're ready.

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 有事请教…
t_en: I'd like to ask something...

t_ch: 嗯？说吧，本姑娘知无不言～
t_en: Hmm? Go ahead. I'll tell you everything I know~

t_ch: 罗浮封锁之事，你知道多少？
t_en: How much do you know about the Luofu being sealed off?

t_ch: 我听说好像哪里发现反物质军团的炸弹…安全起见，整座罗浮都要封锁，把炸弹清除了才能开放。不知道是不是真的。
t_en: I heard they found an Antimatter Legion bomb... Out of safety, they locked down the entire ship. They'll only re-open it after they've removed the bomb... I don't know if all that's true, though.

t_ch: 反正上面啥也没说，就让我过来疏散流云渡里的百姓啦。
t_en: Anyway, the higher-ups didn't say anything — they just got me to evacuate the civilians at Cloudford.

t_ch: 你和这个罗刹之前认识吗？
t_en: Had you met this Luocha before?

t_ch: 不认识啊，遇见他和遇见你，这不是同时嘛。
t_en: Nope, I met him when I met you.

t_ch: 不过你这么一问，好像我还真在哪里见过他？有点眼熟……
t_en: But now that you mention it... maybe I did see him somewhere? He seems a bit familiar...

t_ch: 是在哪里呢……
t_en: Where would that have been...

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 谈谈你吧。
t_en: Let's talk about you.

t_ch: 我？我没什么可说的呀……
t_en: Me? There's not much to talk about...

t_ch: 你加入云骑军多久了？
t_en: When did you join the Cloud Knights?

t_ch: 没多久…但本姑娘从小就接受严格的云骑式训练了！
t_en: Not that long ago... but I've been receiving strict Cloud Knight military training since I was a child!

t_ch: 我爹我娘都是「曜青」的名人！…要不然也不会有我了，所以打我落生起，他俩就特别严格地教育我。
t_en: My parents are both famous on the Yaoqing! I wouldn't be here otherwise. They've been training me ever since I was born!

t_ch: 你可不要因为我是新兵就小瞧我哦～
t_en: Don't look down on me just because I'm a new recruit~

t_ch: 你是「曜青」人？
t_en: You're from the Yaoqing?

t_ch: 对啊，你去过吗？
t_en: Yep. Have you been there before?

t_ch: 我觉得「曜青」和「罗浮」各有各的特色，虽说现在我是罗浮云骑了，但还是觉得曜青好点…就拿领导来说吧，我觉得景元将军就没有我们曜青的女将军靠谱……
t_en: I think the Yaoqing and the Luofu have different characteristics. I might be a Luofu Cloud Knight now, but I still think the Yaoqing is better... For example, when it comes to leadership, I don't think General Jing Yuan is as reliable as our madam general back on the Yaoqing...

t_ch: 呃，把上一句话忘掉吧，就当我没说。
t_en: Uh, a—actually, forget about that last sentence. Just pretend I never said it.

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: Let me have a think first...

t_ch: 好吧，你快点哦。
t_en: Okay, but think fast.

t_ch: 怎么样，能走了吗？
t_en: Well? Can we move out?

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 有事请教…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 嗯？说吧，本姑娘知无不言～
t_en: Hmm? Go ahead. I'll tell you everything I know~

t_ch: 罗浮封锁之事，你知道多少？
t_en: How much do you know about the Luofu being sealed off?

t_ch: 我听说好像哪里发现反物质军团的炸弹…安全起见，整座罗浮都要封锁，把炸弹清除了才能开放。不知道是不是真的。
t_en: I heard that they found an Antimatter Legion bomb, and the entire Luofu needs to be locked down for safety's sake. They'll only re-open after they've removed the bomb... I don't know if that's true.

t_ch: 反正上面啥也没说，就让我过来疏散流云渡里的百姓啦。
t_en: Anyways, the higher-ups didn't say anything. They just got me to come evacuate the civilians in Cloudford.

t_ch: 你和这个罗刹之前认识吗？
t_en: Do you know this man, Luocha?

t_ch: 不认识啊，遇见他和遇见你，这不是同时嘛。
t_en: Nope. I met him and you at the same time.

t_ch: 不过你这么一问，好像我还真在哪里见过他？有点眼熟……
t_en: But now that you've asked... maybe I did see him somewhere? He seems a bit familiar...

t_ch: 是在哪里呢……
t_en: Where would it be...

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 谈谈你吧。
t_en: Let's talk about you.

t_ch: 我？我没什么可说的呀……
t_en: Me? There's not much to talk about...

t_ch: 你加入云骑军多久了？
t_en: When did you join the Cloud Knights?

t_ch: 没多久…但本姑娘从小就接受严格的云骑式训练了！
t_en: Not that long ago... but I've been receiving strict Cloud Knight military training since I was a child!

t_ch: 我爹我娘都是「曜青」的名人！…要不然也不会有我了，所以打我落生起，他俩就特别严格地教育我。
t_en: My parents are both famous on the Yaoqing! ...I wouldn't be here otherwise. They've been training me ever since I was born!

t_ch: 你可不要因为我是新兵就小瞧我哦～
t_en: Don't look down on me just because I'm a new recruit~

t_ch: 你是「曜青」人？
t_en: You're from the Yaoqing?

t_ch: 对啊，你去过吗？
t_en: Yep. Have you been there before?

t_ch: 我觉得「曜青」和「罗浮」各有各的特色，虽说现在我是罗浮云骑了，但还是觉得曜青好点…就拿领导来说吧，我觉得景元将军就没有我们曜青的女将军靠谱……
t_en: I feel the Yaoqing and the Luofu have different characteristics. I might be a Luofu Cloud Knight now, but I still think the Yaoqing is better... For example, when it comes to leadership, I don't feel General Jing Yuan is as reliable as our madam general back on the Yaoqing...

t_ch: 呃，把上一句话忘掉吧，就当我没说。
t_en: Um, actually, forget about that last sentence. Just pretend I never said it.

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: One second.

t_ch: 好吧，你快点哦。
t_en: Okay. Please be quick!

t_ch: 我在前面开路，辛苦二位也留神四周啦。
t_en: I'll lead the way. You two keep an eye on our surroundings.

t_ch: 在下只是一介行商……
t_en: Not exactly my expertise. I'm just a traveling merchant...

t_ch: 事关生命耶！你这人长得也算一表人才，怎么性格这么懒啊！
t_en: This is life and death, okay!? You look like you can handle yourself, how come you're so lazy?

t_ch: 长相和性格，也没有必然联系吧。
t_en: Bold of you to assume my appearance betrays my nature.

t_ch: 闷葫芦，你怎么不吭声啊？发现危险就大声叫唤喔，可别仗着自己懂两下子，就逞血气之勇单打独斗啊！
t_en: Hey, silent but deadly, shout if you see any danger, okay? Don't think just because you know a move or two you can charge off by yourself!

t_ch: 咦，小素裳，你怎么也来了——
t_en: Huh? Sushang? What are you doing here...?

t_ch: 奉命过来疏散群众啊，瞧，我身后这几位就是。我得赶紧带他们出去，这里面好危险啊。
t_en: I have orders to evacuate civilians. See these two behind me? I need to get them out of here quickly. It's too dangerous.

t_ch: 可不是吗。有同僚在附近遭遇了怪物，受伤了，刚刚才救出去。
t_en: You can say that again — some comrades were injured by monsters in the vicinity. They only just made it out.

t_ch: 我奉命殿后，马上也要撤离了，你也快点走吧。
t_en: I'm taking up the rear — I should be out of here soon. You guys should hurry.

t_ch: 打扰，请问你有没有见过三个化外民，一个戴眼镜很稳重……
t_en: Excuse me, have you seen three outworlders? One of them's older — wears glasses — calm demeanor?

t_ch: 不清楚，戴眼镜的人很多。
t_en: Sorry, that could be anyone around here...

t_ch: 有一个粉头发，咋咋呼呼的……
t_en: Then... how about an excitable girl with pink hair...?

t_ch: 噢，我知道你说的是谁，见过，他们已经走了，就是这条道。
t_en: Oh, I know who you're talking about. I saw them leave — they were going this way.

t_ch: …多谢。
t_en: ...Many thanks.

t_ch: 不对啊，这儿应该有条通道的…怎么不见啦？
t_en: There's supposed to be a passage... Why can't I see it?

t_ch: 有人动了流云渡的操纵台，用货箱堵住了路。
t_en: Somebody used the Cloudford control panel to block the road with containers.

t_ch: 谁干的好事啊！这下完了，本姑娘对机巧玩意儿一窍不通…等会儿，你为什么会知道？！
t_en: Ughh, who did this? I'm terrible with machines and stuff... Hold on, how do you know!?

t_ch: 我是行商啊，来过这里很多次了。倒是你一个云骑，好像全不熟悉本地的路线似的。
t_en: I'm a traveling merchant, I've been here many times. Now, for a Cloud Knight to be unfamiliar with the local topography...

t_ch: …都说了我刚从「曜青」仙舟调过来嘛，确实没怎么来过这儿。
t_en: ...I told you, I was only just transferred here from the Yaoqing. I don't come here very often.

t_ch: 操作台，操作台…在哪儿呢？
t_en: Control panel... Where is the control panel?

t_ch: 那边是吗？
t_en: Is that the one?

t_ch: 我看看…哇，罗刹！你眼神真好！
t_en: Let me see... Whoa, Luocha! You've got sharp eyes!

t_ch: 罗刹明明都看见操作台了，为啥我们还找了这么久……
t_en: Luocha already saw the control panel — why is it taking this long to find it...

t_ch: 需要再给你指一下路吗？
t_en: Do you need me to point the way for you again?

t_ch: 咦，可以吗？你真～好。
t_en: Can you? You're toooo kind~

t_ch: 太好啦，总算还有一艘星槎。
t_en: Phew, thank goodness, there's one starskiff left.

t_ch: 这船是通往哪里的？
t_en: Wh—Where does it go?

t_ch: 看码头的线路，终点是星槎海的中枢。嗯，到那儿我们就安全啦。
t_en: Judging by where it's docked, it must be heading for Central Starskiff Haven. Nice, we'll be safe there.

t_ch: 走，咱们上船！
t_en: Come on, let's climb aboard!

t_ch: 加紧动身吧。
t_en: Let's hurry.

t_ch: 有事请教…
t_en: I'd like to ask something...

t_ch: 请说。
t_en: Please do...

t_ch: 你很熟悉流云渡的操纵机制吗？
t_en: Are you familiar with the operational mechanisms in Cloudford?

t_ch: 不清楚，只是见多了几次吧。
t_en: Not really, I've just seen them before, that's all.

t_ch: 你不想去星槎海吗？
t_en: Do you not want to go to Starskiff Haven?

t_ch: 咦？丹恒兄弟何出此言？能离开这儿就很好了，去哪里都行呀。
t_en: Oh? What makes you ask that, young master? I'm happy as long as I get to leave this place.

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 谈谈你吧。
t_en: Let's talk about you.

t_ch: 我是个没什么意思的人呢……
t_en: I'm not a very interesting person...

t_ch: 你接下来有什么打算？
t_en: What are your plans from here?

t_ch: 事已至此，只好走一步看一步了。
t_en: At this stage, I'm just taking it one step at a time.

t_ch: 好在我作为注册行商，在仙舟吃穿住行都有优惠的政策。待久一点也没关系，哈哈。
t_en: Thankfully, as a registered merchant on the Xianzhou, I get a discount on all my expenses. In that regard, it makes sense for me to stay a little longer, haha.

t_ch: 而且那位素裳姑娘说如果我没法离港，她可以带我四处走走呢。
t_en: Miss Sushang said she could show me around if I find myself unable to leave.

t_ch: 你原本要去哪里？
t_en: Where were you going?

t_ch: 手头还有几笔其它仙舟的商单，但…说实话，原本约了「鸣火」商团的停云女士谈笔买卖，看来现在是不成了。
t_en: I had some more trading to do on the other Xianzhou ships, but my main appointment was with Miss Tingyun of the Whistling Flames merchant guild. We had a business deal to discuss, but I fear it will no longer go ahead.

t_ch: 已有的单子没什么利润，都得过公司一道，扣个七七八八。只有一笔生意是直接去「方壶」的，兴许有点价值……
t_en: Most of the deals won't be particularly profitable — the IPC takes the majority. There's only one that might be worth the journey — a direct trade with the Fanghu...

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 没事，叨扰了。
t_en: Never mind. Excuse me.

t_ch: 不会。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 咱们赶紧乘上星槎吧！
t_en: Let's hurry and get on the starskiff!

t_ch: 有事请教…
t_en: I'd like to ask something...

t_ch: 嗯？还有什么事？
t_en: Hmm? Something else?

t_ch: 变换了路线不要紧吗？
t_en: Is it okay to change our route?

t_ch: 不知道耶…我想应该不要紧吧，总之先出去，到了安全的地方怎么都好说。
t_en: I dunno... I think we should be okay. Let's get out of here first and talk about it when we're in the clear.

t_ch: 到了星槎海会怎么样？
t_en: What will happen at Starskiff Haven?

t_ch: 那里应该会有专门的疏散部队接应，然后你跟他们走就好啦。
t_en: There should be a dedicated evacuation team waiting for us. You can go with them.

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 谈谈你吧。
t_en: Let's talk about you.

t_ch: 这都什么时候了……
t_en: Uh, not the best time...?

t_ch: 谢谢你一路引领我们。
t_en: Thank you for guiding us all the way here.

t_ch: 客气什么，这是云骑军人应该做的！
t_en: No need to thank me, just a Cloud Knight doing her job!

t_ch: 接下来你有什么计划？
t_en: What are your plans after this?

t_ch: 呃，得看上面有什么安排，如果还有未疏散的民众，我还得再回来。
t_en: Umm, that depends on the higher-ups. If there are still civilians to evacuate, then I'll need to come back.

t_ch: 还有别的问题…
t_en: I have other questions...

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: Let me have a think first...

t_ch: 好吧，你快点哦。
t_en: Okay, but think fast.

t_ch: 离开流云渡吗？
t_en: Leave Cloudford now?

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 稍等。
t_en: Not yet.

t_ch: 发送失败？怎么回事啊？
t_en: Message not sent? Why not?

t_ch: 不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 信号弱？
t_en: Bad signal?

t_ch: 基站拆了？
t_en: Maybe they demolished the base stations?

t_ch: 超距通信技术还不太成熟，也可能星核的存在干扰了信号传输吧。
t_en: Long-range communication technology is still improving. Perhaps the presence of the Stellaron is affecting signal transmission.

t_ch: 不会呀，其它功能还能用，瞧？
t_en: Doesn't seem like it, everything else is working fine — see?

t_ch: 那么，只可能是有人动了手脚。既然卡芙卡在，想必这应该是星核猎手那个小骇客的手笔吧。
t_en: In that case, it looks like someone wants to make things difficult for us. Since Kafka's here, I wouldn't be surprised if this turns out to be that Stellaron Hunter hacker girl's handiwork.

t_ch: 哎呀，你们来啦。
t_en: Oh, you've arrived.

t_ch: 抱歉，让你久等了。
t_en: Sorry to keep you waiting.

t_ch: 不会，也就多等了半个时辰吧～
t_en: No, no, only an extra couple of hours~

t_ch: 怎么感觉你很失望呢。
t_en: Seems like you're not particularly happy to see us.

t_ch: 恩公说笑了，小女子还盼着一同抓捕星核猎手，立个大功哩～
t_en: Hee-hee, I like your sense of humor, benefactors. Of course I'm happy to see you! When things settle down, I might even get promoted for catching a Stellaron Hunter.

t_ch: 「迴星港」是工造司治下之地，报备许可花了些时间。因此我顺路打听了点情况。
t_en: Stargazer Navalia is governed by the Artisanship Commission — it took me a while to sort out our paperwork. I got a sense of the situation on my way back.

t_ch: 和「流云渡」一样，「迴星港」的穹仪也停转了，关哨失守。云骑军忙着排查星核的位置，无暇他顾。
t_en: Just like at Cloudford, Stargazer Navalia's meridian pin has malfunctioned. The checkpoint there is abandoned and the garrison is scrambling to locate the Stellaron.

t_ch: 内里怪物横行，人迹了无，倒确是藏匿行迹的妙处。
t_en: With monsters wreaking havoc and the Knights scattered, Stargazer Navalia has become a perfect place for the suspect to hide out.

t_ch: 那就是说咱们没帮手，没支援，得对付一群怪物，还得抓个公司悬赏天文数字的星核猎手…行吧，也不是第一次了。
t_en: So... we've got a bunch of monsters to deal with, an IPC most-wanted Stellaron Hunter to catch, and we have to do all that on our own without reinforcements... Fair enough, this isn't our first rodeo.

t_ch: 这不正是我们的日常吗？
t_en: That's just what we do every day.

t_ch: 哈哈——好苦涩哦。
t_en: Haha, that's the trailblazing life for ya.

t_ch: 你以前也抓过星核猎手？
t_en: You've caught a Stellaron Hunter before?

t_ch: 随便聊聊天，不要在意细节！
t_en: I was just making small talk, never mind the details!

t_ch: 呵呵，若说没有援手，也不尽然。恩公们准备好了，咱们就出发吧～
t_en: I wouldn't say you're entirely on your own, hehe... When you're ready, let's get going~

t_ch: 恩公，出发吗？
t_en: Shall we, my benefactors?

t_ch: 动身。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: One second.

t_ch: 我带了一位帮手，定能帮助各位。搜捕卡芙卡兹事体大，天舶司担不起让她走脱了的责任。所以我使了些面子，从工造司那儿借来一个追逃的宝贝。
t_en: Now, what's a hunt without a hound? Kafka is still at large, and the Sky-Faring Commission wants this problem to go away. I made a few calls and managed to procure this from the Artisanship Commission — perfect for hunting down suspects.

t_ch: 这……好可爱！！！
t_en: Awwww he's so CUTE!!!

t_ch: 咳，这什么宝贝？它能干嘛，扑上去咬紧卡芙卡不放么？
t_en: Uh, ahem, I mean... what can he do exactly? Are we gonna set him loose on Kafka?

t_ch: 是，也不是。这是工造司仿生我狐族五感而开发出的「谛听」，敏锐程度更甚。
t_en: Yes, and no. This is Diting, a bionic dog developed by the Artisanship Commission. He has all the five senses of the foxian race and takes them to another level.

t_ch: 无论脚印还是气味，只要锁定特征，它就能嗅探出目标留下的痕迹，一路追查到底。
t_en: Be it tracks or scent, as long as he uncovers a trace, he'll follow it all the way to the end.

t_ch: 只要能找到一点点卡芙卡的痕迹就够了，就算她躲藏得再隐秘，也逃不过「谛听」的追踪。
t_en: All we need to do is look for any trace left by Kafka. It doesn't matter how good she is at hiding, Diting will find her.

t_ch: 不过，在我们正式搜捕前，得先调试一下这个小玩意儿，让它熟悉工作～
t_en: But before we get started, let's play with his settings a bit and get him ready~

t_ch: 循着这些亮晶晶的痕迹，把停云小姐找出来对吧？
t_en: *sigh* We need to follow these sparkly traces to find Miss Tingyun, right?

t_ch: 停云小姐也真是的，这么紧要的关头还和咱们玩起捉迷藏来了。
t_en: She's not in a hurry, that's for sure. Playing hide-and-seek with us while Kafka's still at large.

t_ch: 首先，把它设置成能嗅探某人留下的气味～
t_en: To begin with, let's set him to scent detection mode~

t_ch: 不妨以我的香水味作为目标，各位拿「谛听」来试试身手吧。
t_en: Tell Diting to follow the scent of my perfume and see what he can do!

t_ch: 痕迹…消失了？是「谛听」失灵了吗？
t_en: The traces... where did they go? Is Diting malfunctioning?

t_ch: 呜汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: 嗯嗯。它说港口风急，要用气味追踪可不容易。
t_en: Got it. He says the wind is too strong here at the port, so it's not easy to follow the scent.

t_ch: 你是怎么听懂的？
t_en: How do you know what he's saying?

t_ch: 它只叫了一声，哪来这么多话？
t_en: That's a lot of words for one bark, don't you think?

t_ch: 是美少女和可爱小狗之间的心灵感应！
t_en: That's just the psychic bond between cute girls and cute puppies!

t_ch: 利用环境掩盖痕迹…天舶司的这位姑娘还真喜欢考验人啊。咱们四处找找，看看她有没有留下别的痕迹？
t_en: Using the environment to hide her traces... Our girl from the Sky-Faring Commission sure knows how to put up a challenge. Let's look around and see if we can find any other clues.

t_ch: 或者，问问附近的人吧？这里人来人往，一定有人看见过停云小姐。这也是收集信息的一种手段，应该不算作弊吧？
t_en: Or... we could ask around? Look how busy this place is, someone must have seen Miss Tingyun. That's not cheating, right? We're just collecting intel.

t_ch: 汪，呜！
t_en: Woof! Woof!

t_ch: 打扰了，请问两位有看见一位狐人经过吗？
t_en: Excuse me, any chance you saw a foxian lady pass by?

t_ch: 这问题可问对人了。星槎海终日狐人来来往往，没个一两千也有七八百。您问的狐人，与我们见过的狐人，那未必是同一个人。
t_en: Well that's a heck of a question, miss! I'd say nearly one in every two people passing through Starskiff Haven is foxian. We might have seen the lady you're looking for, but we wouldn't have batted an eyelid.

t_ch: 那肯定不是。要不您给咱形容形容？
t_en: He's not lying. Still, if you describe her a little, maybe it'll jog our memory.

t_ch: 是个…狐人？
t_en: She's... foxian?

t_ch: 好么，这一句话就抓准了问题的关键。
t_en: Write that down, write that down!

t_ch: 是个…漂亮姑娘。
t_en: She's... pretty.

t_ch: 「好看」这个形容词放在狐族身上有些宽泛。莫说女子，便是肤白貌美的狐人男子也一抓一大把……
t_en: "Pretty?" That's too generic for a foxian. Even if you don't count the ladies, there are plenty of gents that fit that description like a glove...

t_ch: 您受累，再展开讲讲，她有没有什么明显特征——头上有耳朵，身后有尾巴可不算。
t_en: As tedious as it is, I'm afraid you'll have to describe her in more detail. Please don't say "two ears, one tail."

t_ch: （展示停云的照片）
t_en: (Show Tingyun's photo.)

t_ch: 这个妹妹我曾见过的。
t_en: ...I can put my hand on my heart and say I remember this fine lady.

t_ch: 你可又胡说，我和你在这站了这么久，何曾见过她？
t_en: Nonsense. I've been stood next to you this whole time and I don't remember anyone like her.

t_ch: 你看这照片，我虽然没有见过她，但是我看她容貌姣好，在我心里就把她当作是旧相识了，今天就是我们久别重逢，
t_en: Well, I may not have seen her in person, but her beauty feels like a long-lost acquaintance... How wonderful to be reunited with her after so long!

t_ch: {F#姑娘}{M#小哥}您听懂了吧，我这搭档是在白日做梦呢。
t_en: Oh dear. I'm sorry, my friend here appears to have lost touch with reality.

t_ch: 你俩是特地来消遣咱们的吧？
t_en: Are you two professional time-wasters or something?

t_ch: 真对不住，咱哥俩平日里在街角的茶馆「不夜侯」搭档讲相声。情况你也看到了。云骑军一戒严，这几天茶馆生意是做不成啦。咱就寻思找个露天的地儿开开嗓子，练练功。
t_en: Please accept our apologies! Me and my partner do double act comedy in the Sleepless Earl — the teahouse round the corner. As you can probably guess, with all these curfews and lockdowns, we've hardly had an audience the past few days. We figured we'd find a spot on the street and do some rehearsal — just to stop us getting rusty.

t_ch: 这不赶巧您来问人嘛，我心想我得露一手活儿啊。您别担心，这是免费表演，不强买强卖。
t_en: Then you guys swung by asking for this foxian lady, so we thought we'd give you a bit of improv — free of charge I might add.

t_ch: 别气啊！您说的这姑娘我刚巧见过。就刚才，我瞧她转进了宣夜大道。您往那儿溜达溜达，快几步兴许还来得及找见。
t_en: Stay calm, stay calm! I did see the girl you're looking for. She took a turn from here into Starwatcher Avenue. I'd suggest you head over that way — she might still be there.

t_ch: 从这两张嘴里说出来的话还能信吗…？
t_en: I find it hard to trust anything you've just said...

t_ch: 被你们找到啦~有了「谛听」，追踪是不是更得心应手了呢？
t_en: You found me~ Diting's quite handy when it comes to tracking, isn't he?

t_ch: 在迴星港中如果发现了卡芙卡的踪迹，就开启这个小东西，别让她跑了。
t_en: If you pick up traces of Kafka in Stargazer Navalia, just switch him on. Don't let her escape.

t_ch: 我想，这几件东西应该和停云小姐都有关吧？
t_en: Miss Tingyun must have left us these clues, right?

t_ch: 现在「谛听」应该能追踪到她的踪迹了。
t_en: Diting should be able to pick up her traces now.

t_ch: 一张洁白的名刺，角落印有一只展翅欲飞的鸢。落款则是「天舶司商团『鸣火』的首席代表，接渡使停云」。
t_en: A piece of white name card, with a bird spreading its wings at a corner. The card belongs to Amicassador Tingyun, Head Representative of the intergalactic merchant guild, Whistling Flames.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，这是她抛给你们的小小线索，之一？
t_en: No doubt this is a tiny clue she left for you. Or maybe there are more of them?
